dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4361 IS: Pets Belong with Families Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Schiff introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To modify the reasonable requirements that may be imposed on pet owners living in public housing, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Pets Belong with Families Act.
Section 31 of the United States Housing Act of 1937 (42 U.S.C. 1437z–3) is amended by striking subsection (b) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: The reasonable requirements referred to in subsection (a) may include—

subparagraph.text: requiring payment of a pet deposit by a tenant owning or having pets, except that—

subclause.text: shall not exceed 10 percent of the tenant’s monthly base rent; and
shall be amortized over not less than 3 months as part of a tenant’s rental payments; and

clause.text: any amount of a pet deposit that is unused at the termination of the tenancy shall be reimbursed to the tenant within 30 days of vacating a dwelling unit;

subparagraph.text: limitations on the number of animals in a dwelling unit or building, based on unit size or other relevant conditions;
prohibitions on certain species of animals prohibited by State or local law; and
prohibitions on any individual animal that is declared a threat to public health and safety by a court of competent jurisdiction or whose ownership is otherwise prohibited by State or local law.

paragraph.text: A public housing agency may not withhold a pet deposit described in paragraph (1) for damage unrelated to the pet, including due to ordinary wear and tear.
The reasonable requirements described in subsection (a) shall not include any prohibition based on the breed, size, or weight of an animal.
